Delhi: India's capital, Red Fort, Qutub Minar and Mughal-era monuments
Jaipur: the Pink City, Hawa Mahal, Amber Fort and gemstone jewellery
Kanpur: leather tanning and the mills that made it the Manchester of the East
Lucknow: Nawabi tehzeeb, chikankari embroidery, Awadhi biryani and Bara Imambara
Ghaziabad: the industrial Gateway of Uttar Pradesh bordering Delhi
Agra: the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort and Mughal marble inlay work
Ludhiana: the hosiery and woollen knitwear capital of India, plus bicycles
Meerut: sports goods, scissors and the 1857 revolt's first spark
Faridabad: a major industrial and manufacturing hub of Haryana
Srinagar: Dal Lake houseboats, shikaras, Mughal gardens and Pashmina shawls
Prayagraj: the Triveni Sangam confluence and the Kumbh Mela
Amritsar: the Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh and hearty Punjabi cuisine
Jodhpur: the Blue City, mighty Mehrangarh Fort and Marwari heritage
Gwalior: its hilltop fort, Scindia palace and Hindustani classical music gharana
Chandigarh: Le Corbusier's planned city and the Rock Garden
Kota: coaching institutes, Kota Doria sarees and Chambal riverfront
Gurugram: India's glass-tower corporate and IT-outsourcing millennium city
Noida: a planned IT, media and corporate satellite city near Delhi
Bareilly: zari-zardozi embroidery, surma, jhumkas and Manjha kite-string
Moradabad: the Brass City, exporting engraved brassware and metal handicrafts worldwide
Aligarh: its lock-making industry and Aligarh Muslim University
Jalandhar: the sports-goods manufacturing capital of India
Gorakhpur: the Gorakhnath temple, Geeta Press and terracotta crafts
Jhansi: its fort and Rani Lakshmibai's 1857 rebellion stand
Ayodhya: the birthplace of Lord Rama and the Ram Mandir
Varanasi: the Ganga ghats, Kashi Vishwanath temple and Banarasi silk
Udaipur: the City of Lakes, Lake Palace and Rajput royal grandeur
Ajmer: the Ajmer Sharif Sufi dargah and Pushkar lake nearby
Bikaner: Junagarh Fort, camel country, bhujia and usta miniature art
Patiala: the Patiala peg, colourful parandas, phulkari and Qila Mubarak
Bathinda: the ancient Qila Mubarak fort and a major rail junction
Mohali: its IT parks and international cricket stadium near Chandigarh
Panipat: the Textile City of handwoven blankets, carpets and durries, plus historic battles
Ambala: India's scientific-instruments and laboratory-glassware manufacturing hub
Jammu: the City of Temples and gateway to the Vaishno Devi shrine
Shimla: the summer capital of the Raj, the Ridge and toy train
Dharamshala: the Dalai Lama's home, McLeod Ganj and Tibetan Buddhist culture
Dehradun: Doon Valley schools, the Forest Research Institute and basmati rice
Haridwar: the Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri and Kumbh Mela
Rishikesh: the Yoga Capital of the World and Ganga river rafting
Nainital: its emerald hill lake and colonial Kumaon hill-station charm
Leh: Ladakh's high desert, Buddhist monasteries and Himalayan landscapes
Kargil: the 1999 war, frigid Drass and gateway to Zanskar
Chennai: Marina Beach, Fort St. George and Tamil cinema
Bengaluru: India's Silicon Valley of IT and garden-city lakes
Hyderabad: the Charminar, Golconda Fort and dum biryani
Coimbatore: cotton textile mills and pump-and-motor manufacturing
Kochi: Chinese fishing nets, spice trade and Fort Kochi
Visakhapatnam: its natural harbour, steel plant and beaches
Vijayawada: the Kanaka Durga temple and Prakasam Barrage on the Krishna
Madurai: the towering Meenakshi Amman temple and jasmine
Thiruvananthapuram: the golden Padmanabhaswamy temple and Kovalam beach
Kozhikode: the spice trade, Malabar cuisine and halwa
Mysuru: its royal palace, Dasara festival and silk sarees
Tiruchirappalli: the Rockfort temple and Srirangam's vast Ranganathaswamy shrine
Hubballi-Dharwad: Dharwad pedha and North Karnataka's commercial hub
Salem: steel, textiles, mangoes and the surrounding hills
Tirunelveli: its iru-halwa sweet and the Nellaiappar temple
Thanjavur: the Brihadisvara temple, Tanjore paintings and Carnatic music
Vellore: the granite Golden Temple and CMC hospital
Mangaluru: its port, Mangalorean cuisine and coastal temples
Belagavi: its historic fort, Kunda sweet and military cantonment
Kalaburagi: toor dal, Bahmani-era tombs and the great fort
Thrissur: the spectacular Thrissur Pooram elephant festival and gold trade
Kollam: cashew processing and the vast Ashtamudi backwaters
Kannur: handloom weaving, Theyyam ritual dance and its seafront fort
Warangal: the Kakatiya Thousand Pillar Temple and Ramappa's carved shrine
Nizamabad: turmeric, red jowar and its historic Nizam-era fort
Guntur: fiery red chillies and tobacco trade
Tirupati: the Venkateswara temple at Tirumala and its laddu
Nellore: paddy rice, aquaculture and the Ranganatha temple
Kurnool: the Belum caves and former Andhra capital heritage
Rajamahendravaram: the Godavari river and Andhra's Telugu cultural capital
Puducherry: French colonial quarter, seafront promenade and Auroville
Karaikal: its French heritage and the Thirunallar Saniswaran temple
Kavaratti: Lakshadweep's capital of turquoise lagoons and coral reefs
Mumbai: Bollywood, the Gateway of India and India's financial capital
Ahmedabad: textile mills, Gandhi's Sabarmati Ashram and stepwells
Pune: education, IT industry and Maratha Peshwa heritage
Surat: diamond cutting and polishing and silk textile trade
Nagpur: oranges, India's geographic zero-mile centre and tiger gateway
Indore: street food, Rajwada palace and India's cleanest city
Bhopal: lakes, Bhimbetka rock shelters and grand mosques
Vadodara: the Laxmi Vilas Palace, art academy and Gaekwad heritage
Nashik: vineyards and wine, Kumbh Mela and Ramayana Panchavati
Rajkot: Gandhi's boyhood home, jewellery and engineering workshops
Jabalpur: the Marble Rocks gorge and Dhuandhar Falls on the Narmada
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar: the Ajanta-Ellora caves, Bibi ka Maqbara and Paithani sarees
Solapur: GI-tagged chaddars, terry towels and beedi rolling
Thane: its many lakes and India's first passenger railway terminus
Navi Mumbai: India's largest planned city and flamingo-filled wetlands
Amravati: the Ambadevi temple, cotton, oranges and Melghat tiger reserve
Kolhapur: leather chappals, jaggery, the Mahalaxmi temple and spicy tambda-pandhra rassa
Bhavnagar: the Alang ship-breaking yard and diamond cutting
Jamnagar: brass parts, bandhani tie-dye and the world's largest oil refinery
Gandhinagar: Gujarat's planned capital and the Akshardham temple
Ujjain: the Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga and the Kumbh Mela on the Shipra
Panaji: Portuguese Latin Quarter Fontainhas and the Mandovi riverfront
Margao: Goa's commercial hub, colonial mansions and the Holy Spirit Church
Vasco da Gama: Goa's largest town and the deep-water Mormugao port
Daman: Portuguese Moti Daman fort, seaside churches and Jampore beach
Silvassa: Warli tribal culture, Portuguese heritage and Vasona lion safari
Kolkata: colonial-era Howrah Bridge, Durga Puja, Bengali literature and sweets
Patna: ancient Pataliputra, Sikh shrine Takht Sri Patna Sahib, Ganges ghats
Jamshedpur: the Tata Steel plant that built India's first planned steel city
Asansol: Raniganj coalfields and its railway and steel industry
Dhanbad: the coal-mining capital of India and its collieries
Ranchi: waterfalls, tribal heartland and cricketer M.S. Dhoni's hometown
Raipur: steel and rice-mill hub, gateway to Chhattisgarh
Bhubaneswar: the temple city of Odisha, Lingaraja shrine and Kalinga architecture
Howrah: the iconic cantilever Howrah Bridge and its railway terminus
Siliguri: gateway to the Northeast, Darjeeling and Himalayan tea trade
Durgapur: steel plant and industrial township, the Ruhr of Bengal
Darjeeling: world-famous tea, the toy-train Himalayan Railway and Kanchenjunga views
Bhilai: the Bhilai Steel Plant, one of India's largest
Gaya: Bodh Gaya's Mahabodhi Temple and Hindu pind-daan pilgrimage
Bhagalpur: Tussar silk weaving, the Silk City on the Ganges
Muzaffarpur: prized Shahi litchi orchards and Bihar's leechi trade
Bokaro Steel City: the Bokaro Steel Plant and its planned township
Bilaspur: the South East Central Railway zone and Kosa silk
Cuttack: the Silver City's filigree tarakasi work and Barabati Fort
Rourkela: India's first public-sector steel plant, the Steel City of Odisha
Sambalpur: Sambalpuri ikat handloom sarees and the Hirakud Dam
Puri: the Jagannath Temple, Rath Yatra chariot festival and its beach
Brahmapur: the Silk City, Berhampuri Patta sarees and phoda-kumbha weaving
Guwahati: the Kamakhya Temple, Brahmaputra riverfront and gateway to Northeast India
Silchar: Barak Valley's tea gardens and its Bengali-language martyrs
Dibrugarh: the Tea City of Assam and Upper Assam oilfields
Jorhat: the Tea Capital of Assam and river-island Majuli's gateway
Itanagar: Ita Fort, Buddhist Gompa and capital of Arunachal Pradesh
Tawang: the great Tawang Monastery, birthplace of the sixth Dalai Lama
Imphal: the Kangla Fort, women-run Ima Keithel market and Loktak lake
Churachandpur: multi-ethnic Zo hill town of caves, gorges and forests
Shillong: pine-clad hills, waterfalls and the Scotland of the East
Tura: Garo Hills capital and the hundred-drums Wangala harvest festival
Aizawl: ridge-top Mizo capital of churches, hills and bamboo culture
Lunglei: the cultural capital of Mizoram, named for a bridge-like rock
Kohima: the WWII war cemetery, Naga Hills and Hornbill Festival
Dimapur: Nagaland's commercial gateway and the ancient Kachari Rajbari ruins
Agartala: the Ujjayanta Palace and Neermahal water palace of Tripura
Dharmanagar: one of Tripura's oldest towns, gateway to Unakoti
Gangtok: Himalayan capital of monasteries, Kanchenjunga views and MG Marg
Namchi: the Char Dham complex and giant Guru Padmasambhava statue
Sri Vijaya Puram: the Cellular Jail Kala Pani and island-hopping capital of the Andamans
